questions.question_text: The most frequent congenital malformation of esophagus is:
A newborn after delivery has hypersalivation. The first feeding attempt (after 2-3 swallows) causes cyanosis and coughing. Dyspnea and polypnea occurs. What is the suspected diagnosis?
The following affirmations concerning esophageal atresia are right, excepting:

answers.text: Esophageal atesia is the most frequent and severe congenital malformation of the esophagus
Esophageal atresia is a fatal disease, because of impossibility to feed the newborn
There are communications between esophagus and trachea in some types of esophageal atresia.
Drooling and excessive oral salivation are the dominant symptoms of esophageal atresia
Vomiting with milk is the dominant symptom of esophageal atresia

questions.question_text: The recurrent pneumonia is more common in the following congenital malformation:

A newborn 5 hours after birth presents progressive expiratory dyspnea, polypnea, cyanosis and coughing. On inspection the left hemithorax is bulged, with horizontal ribs and widened intercostal spaces. On percussion of the affected side tympanic resonance is present and on auscultation the vesicular respiration is absent. The abdomen is without any findings. What is the suspected diagnosis?
Which of the examinations below is of most value in the diagnosis of congenital lobar emphysema?
The hypertrophic pyloric stenosis is a:

answers.text: High bowel obstruction at the level of papilla Vateri
Low bowel obstruction at the level of pylorus
High bowel obstruction at the level of pylorus due to hypertrophy of mucosa
High bowel obstruction at the level of pylorus due to muscle hypertrophy
Low bowel obstruction at the level of pylorus due to muscle hypertrophy

questions.question_text: The main symptom of hypertrophic pyloric stenosis is:

answers.text: Projectile vomiting with food (milk or formula), the vomiting volume exceeds the last meal amount

questions.question_text: In hypertrophic pyloric stenosis the vomiting occurs:

answers.text: Up to the age of 1 year

questions.question_text: A firm, painless on palpation, olive shaped tumor in the right hypochondrium, in the pancreato-duodenal area in a child of 5 weeks, who have projectile milky vomiting from the age of 3 weeks, is present in:

answers.text: Incomplete duodenal obstruction by a perforated web

questions.question_text: The bilious vomiting from the birth is a dominant symptom in:
The ultrasound sign characteristic for hypertrophic pyloric stenosis is:
The following affirmations about Ladd syndrome are true, excepting:

answers.text: Ladd syndrome is a congenital extrinsic duodenal obstruction
In Ladd syndrome the ascendant colon is not fixed in the right gutter
In Ladd syndrome the ascendant colon remains hanged in place of colon transversum
In Ladd syndrome the parietocolic ligament (Ladd's bands) compresses the second part of the duodenum
In Ladd syndrome the parietocolic ligament (Ladd's bands) compresses the ileum

questions.question_text: The meconeal ileus is a consequence of:
The key role in the pathophysiology of Hirschsprung disease plays the following:
The following affirmations as far as congenital megacolon (Hirschsprung disease) is concerned are true excepting:

answers.text: The positive diagnostic is established on the base of clinical signs, anorectal manometry, barium enema and rectal biopsy.
The aganglionosis (lack of Auerbach's and Meissner's plexus ganglia) plays the key role in the pathophysiology of Hirschsprung disease.
Severe cases of Hirschsprung disease are self curing.
At the anorectal manometry the rectoanal inhibitory reflex is absent.
In mild cases of Hirschsprung disease the constipations occur after several months from the birth when the alimentary diversification in attempted.

questions.question_text: The following affirmations in a child with congenital megacolon are true:

answers.text: Diagnosis is established on the base of clinical signs
Diagnosis is established on the base of rectal examination
Diagnosis is established on the base of ultrasound examination
Diagnosis is established on the base of clinical signs, clinical findings, anorectal manometry, barium enema and rectal biopsy.
Diagnosis is established on the base of barium enema

questions.question_text: The commonest type of intussusception in an infant is:
The intermittent, crampy abdominal pain associated with recurrent vomiting, "currant jelly" stools and a palpable abdominal mass are the symtoms triad in:

A child of 7 months is admitted after 13 hours from the sudden disease onset against the background of full wellbeing. At the admission he presented colicky abdominal pains, recurrent vomiting. The child is agitated and refuses the food. The agitation periods alternate with periods of quietness. The abdomen is of normal shape, soft, elastic and slightly painful. In the subepatic area an elongated, cylindrical, elastic mass, with smooth surface is palpated. The stools are absent from the onset. What is the suspected diagnosis?
The definitive diagnosis of the bowel intussusception in an older child is based on:

answers.text: Physical examination, plain abdominal X-ray film, air enema, abdominal ultrasound
Only on the base of plain abdominal X-ray
Only on the base of air enema
Only on the base of abdominal ultrasound

questions.question_text: The following affirmations as far as hemangiomas are concerned are true excepting:

answers.text: Hemangiomas are vascular tumors which consist of abnormal vessels
Hemangiomas have a potential of dramatic progression in size in the first 6 months of life
In 70-75% of cases hemangiomas are self curing tumors and don't need any treatment
Hemangiomas complications include hemorrhage and infection
Hemangiomas are located only on the face

questions.question_text: The following affirmations concerning branchial cleft cysts are true, excepting:

answers.text: Branchial cleft cysts are congenital epithelial cysts, which arise on the lateral part of the neck from a failure of obliteration of the second branchial cleft in embryonic development
Branchial cysts are smooth, nontender, fluctuant masses, which occur along the lower one third of the anteromedial border of the sternocleidomastoid muscle between the muscle and the overlying skin
When associated with a sinus tract, mucoid or purulent discharge onto the skin or into the pharynx may be present
The branchial cleft cyst is a congenital lesion formed by incomplete involution of the thyroglossal duct

Complications of surgical excision of branchial cleft cysts result from damage to nearby vascular or neural structures, which include carotid vessels and the facial, hypoglossal, vagus, and lingual nerves

questions.question_text: The commonest complication of acute appendicitis in children is:
The main symptom of esophageal atresia is:
Which of the examinations below is of most value in the diagnosis of esophageal atresia?

answers.text: Thoraco-abdominal plain X-ray in the upright position
Thoraco-abdominal plain X-ray in the upright position with contrasting probe introduced in the esophagus
Thoraco-abdominal X-ray with contrast substance (Upper gastrointestinal series)

questions.question_text: The main symptom of tracheo-esophageal fistula (H fistula) is:

answers.text: Noisy respiration, dyspnea from the birth
Recurrent coughing during feeding (the coughing interrupts sucking)

questions.question_text: The cause of generalized cyanosis in neonate is:
The following affirmations as far as congenital lobar emphysema is concerned are wrong, excepting:

answers.text: Congenital lobar emphysema is an asymptomatic disease
The dominant symptom of congenital lobar emphysema is sepsis
The dominant symptoms of congenital lobar emphysema are dyspnea, polypnea and progressive cyanosis
The dominant symptom of congenital lobar emphysema is coughing with mucopurulent expectorations.
In children with congenital lobar emphysema chest X-ray reveals a round shaped, air-liquid shadow on the affected side.

questions.question_text: Which of the examinations below is of most value in the diagnosis of congenital diaphragmatic hernia?

answers.text: Without any consequences as far as body weight gain is concerned

questions.question_text: Milky projectile, explosive vomiting at the age of 6 weeks is permanently present in a child with:
The body weight of a child with hypertrophic pyloric stenosis:
The main radiographic sign of hypertrophic pyloric stenosis is:

answers.text: Elongated, filiform, centrally located pyloric canal ("string sign")
Strong gastric waves along greater and lesser curvatures of the stomach

questions.question_text: The dominant symptom in the duodenal atresia is:
The presence of 2 air-liquid levels in the upper abdomen (on the opposite sites of the spine) on the thoraco-abdominal plain X-ray film in the upright position is a sign of:
The bowel aganglionosis in Hirschsprung disease leads to:

answers.text: Excessive peristaltic waves in the aganglionic segment
Absence of peristaltic waves in the aganglionic segment
Absence of the peristaltic waves at the level of small bowel

questions.question_text: Most cases of primary intussusception occur in children at the age of:

answers.text: In the first 7 days of life
In the first hours of life

questions.question_text: The following affirmations regarding thyroglossal cyst are true, excepting:

answers.text: A thyroglossal cyst is a fibrous cyst that forms from a persistent thyroglossal duct
It usually presents as a midline neck lump (in the region of the hyoid bone) that is usually painless, smooth and cystic
The most common locations for a thyroglossal cyst are midline or slightly off midline, between the isthmus of the thyroid and the hyoid bone or just above the hyoid bone
A thyroglossal cyst will not move upwards with protrusion of the tongue

questions.question_text: Choose the disease in which the newborn has respiratory distress and the mouth is full of salivary secretions:

The main symptom of the onset of hypertrophic pyloric stenosis is:
The dominant symptom of the duodenal atresia is:
The clinical symptom of the congenital high bowel obstruction is:

answers.text: Bilious vomiting, lack of meconial stools
Meconial and then fecaloid vomiting, lack of meconial stools

questions.question_text: The newborn on plain abdominal X-ray has two hydroaeric levels (“double bubble sign”) (on the contralateral sides of spine). What is the diagnosis?

answers.text: Duodenal atresia after the papilla Vateri

questions.question_text: The commonest cause of the bilious vomiting in a newborn is:
The dominant symptom of congenital high bowel obstruction is:

answers.text: Alimentary vomiting with traces of blood

questions.question_text: The dominant symptom of congenital low bowel obstruction is:

answers.text: Bilious vomiting from the first 24 hours of life
Bilious vomiting from the 24-48 hours of life and then fecaloid vomiting
Very tenacious meconium adherent to the intestinal wall.

questions.question_text: What disease should be mandatory ruled in a newborn with bilious vomiting?

answers.text: Congenital duodenal or small bowel obstruction

questions.question_text: The causes that contribute to the development of bowel intussusception in infants are:

answers.text: Morphologic and functional immaturity of the bowel wall
Disturbances in the regulation of peristaltic activity

questions.question_text: The characteristic clinical signs of bowel intussusception in infants include:

answers.text: Stool with blood and mucus ("currant jelly" stool)

questions.question_text: The symptoms included in Alvarado Score for appendicitis are:

answers.text: Migration of the pain to the right iliac fossa
Tenderness in the right iliac fossa, Rebound pain
Elevated temperature (fever), Leukocytosis, Shift to the left of neutrophils

questions.question_text: The acute appendicitis in a child of up to 3 years manifests itself by the following symptoms:

answers.text: Local pain in the right lower quadrant
Local pain in the paraumbelical region
Rebound tenderness in the right lower quadrant

questions.question_text: A neonate with jejunoileal atresia shows the following:

answers.text: Gray mucus plugs passed via rectum

questions.question_text: The following is true in a newborn with duodenal atresia:

answers.text: Clear or bilious vomiting within hours of birth is present
An output of more then 5 ml of gastric content is indicative for positive diagnosis
An output of more then 20 ml of gastric content is indicative for positive diagnosis
Blood may be passed via rectum

answers.text: Full-thickness rectal biopsy or rectal suction biopsy

questions.question_text: First degree burns are usually:
Second degree burns are usually:
Third degree burns are usually:

answers.text: Extending through entire skin, and into underlying fat, muscle and bone

questions.question_text: The traumatic injury of the spleen is diagnosed by:

answers.text: One stage reduction under general anesthesia

answers.text: One stage reduction under general anesthesia

answers.text: One stage reduction under general anesthesia

answers.text: One stage reduction under general anesthesia

Decreased abduction on the affected side

questions.question_text: The main symptoms of clubfoot are:

answers.text: One stage reduction under general anesthesia

questions.question_text: The congenital clubfoot should be differentiated from:

questions.question_text: Choose the symptoms of omphalocele:

answers.text: Herniation of abdominal organs through a very enlarged umbilical ring
Exteriorization of the small bowel through a paraombelical abdominal wall defect
Eviscerated organs are covered by avascular membrane
Eviscerated intestinal loops are retracted and edematous.
The belly button is of normal appearance

questions.question_text: The symptoms of gastroschisis are as follows:

answers.text: Herniation of abdominal organs through a very enlarged umbilical ring

On palpation the testicle is enlarged and in a higher position

questions.question_text: The following affirmations regarding hemangiomas are true:

answers.text: The natural evolution of hemangioma is towards spontaneous resolution

questions.question_text: Symptoms of congenital hip dysplasia are as follows:

External rotation of the involved extremity

Limitation of the adduction in the involved hip joint

questions.question_text: Choose the specific symptoms of perforated ulcerative-necrotic enterocolitis in an infant:

Lack of intestinal transit for gases and fecal matter

questions.question_text: Choose the right affirmations regarding acute appendicitis in children:

answers.text: Is the most seldom surgical pathology of childhood
Is the most frequent surgical pathology of childhood
